name: Medical Symptom & Diagnosis
description: Comprehensive clinical decision support covering 7 body systems — cardiovascular, neurological, gastrointestinal, respiratory, musculoskeletal, dermatological, and systemic — with triage, differential diagnosis, lab/imaging interpretation, medication review, and feedback loops for treatment monitoring

defaultWeights.label: Patient Safety
defaultWeights.description: Conservative vs aggressive approach — high value errs on caution, orders more tests, and assumes worst-case differentials first

defaultWeights.label: Diagnostic Urgency
defaultWeights.description: How quickly a diagnosis and intervention are needed — high value expedites workup and lowers the threshold for emergency action

defaultWeights.label: Test Invasiveness
defaultWeights.description: Preference for non-invasive testing first — high value starts with labs and imaging before biopsies or scopes

defaultWeights.label: Cost Consciousness
defaultWeights.description: Patient financial burden consideration — high value suggests cost-effective alternatives (generics, outpatient vs inpatient, fewer panels)

defaultWeights.label: Differential Breadth
defaultWeights.description: How many possible diagnoses to consider simultaneously — high casts a wide net, low focuses on the most probable diagnosis

defaultWeights.label: Evidence Strength
defaultWeights.description: Reliance on evidence-based guidelines (UpToDate, ACC/AHA, NICE) — high follows protocols strictly, low relies on clinical judgment

defaultWeights.label: Specialist Threshold
defaultWeights.description: When to refer to a specialist — high refers early for complex presentations, low manages more in primary care

defaultWeights.label: Holistic Care
defaultWeights.description: Consider psychosocial factors, mental health, lifestyle, and social determinants — high includes screening for anxiety, depression, and social support

nodes.label: Primary Symptom Category
nodes.description: Select the main body system or symptom area of concern
nodes.prompt: What is the primary symptom or body system affected?
choices: Chest / Cardiovascular — chest pain, palpitations, shortness of breath, edema
Neurological — headache, dizziness, numbness, tingling, seizures, vision changes
Gastrointestinal — abdominal pain, nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, constipation, blood in stool
Respiratory — cough, wheezing, difficulty breathing, sputum production
Musculoskeletal — joint pain, back pain, swelling, stiffness, limited mobility
Dermatological — rash, itching, skin lesions, discoloration, wound concerns
General / Systemic — fever, fatigue, weight changes, night sweats, malaise

nodes.label: Symptom Duration & Onset
nodes.description: When did the symptom start and how quickly did it develop
nodes.prompt: How long have you had this symptom and how did it start?
choices: Sudden onset — seconds to minutes (acute emergency consideration)
Acute — started within the last 24 hours
Chronic — present for more than 1 week
Recurrent — comes and goes repeatedly over weeks/months

nodes.label: Patient Demographics
nodes.description: Age, sex, height, weight, and relevant demographic information
nodes.prompt: Provide the patient's age, biological sex, height, and weight (e.g., '45-year-old male, 5'10", 185 lbs')

nodes.label: Pain Severity (0–10)
nodes.description: Rate pain intensity on the standard numeric rating scale
nodes.prompt: Rate the pain severity on a scale from 0 (no pain) to 10 (worst imaginable pain)

nodes.label: Vital Signs
nodes.description: Current vital signs including blood pressure, heart rate, temperature, respiratory rate, and oxygen saturation
nodes.prompt: Enter the patient's vital signs: Blood Pressure (e.g., 120/80), Heart Rate (bpm), Temperature (°F or °C), Respiratory Rate (breaths/min), SpO2 (%)

nodes.label: Medical History & Medications
nodes.description: Relevant past medical history, surgical history, current medications, and known allergies
nodes.prompt: Describe the patient's medical history, including: past diagnoses, surgeries, current medications (with doses if known), and any known allergies

nodes.label: Associated Symptoms
nodes.description: Other symptoms occurring alongside the primary complaint
nodes.prompt: What other symptoms are present? (e.g., fever, nausea, sweating, anxiety, loss of appetite, swollen lymph nodes)

nodes.label: Lifestyle & Social History
nodes.description: Smoking status, alcohol use, exercise habits, diet, stress level, occupation
nodes.prompt: Describe relevant lifestyle factors: smoking (pack-years), alcohol use, exercise frequency, diet, stress level, occupation, and recent travel

nodes.label: Clinical Image Upload
nodes.description: Upload a photo of the affected area — skin lesion, rash, swelling, wound, or any visible finding
nodes.prompt: Upload a clinical image if applicable (skin lesion, rash, swelling, wound, X-ray, or any visible finding)

nodes.label: No Immediate Life Threat
nodes.description: Assumes patient is not in immediate cardiopulmonary arrest or anaphylaxis
nodes.assumption: The patient is conscious, breathing, and hemodynamically stable enough for a structured evaluation. If the patient is unresponsive, in respiratory failure, or showing signs of anaphylaxis/cardiac arrest, this tree should be bypassed for immediate emergency protocols (BLS/ACLS).

nodes.label: Medication Compliance
nodes.description: Assumes the patient is taking prescribed medications as directed
nodes.assumption: The patient has been compliant with their current medication regimen. Non-compliance may be a significant cause of symptom onset or worsening (e.g., missed antihypertensives, skipped insulin, stopped anticoagulants). If false, medication non-adherence should be considered as a primary contributing factor.

nodes.label: Accurate Self-Reporting
nodes.description: Assumes the patient is reporting symptoms accurately and honestly
nodes.assumption: The patient is providing an accurate and honest account of their symptoms, duration, and severity. In some cases (pediatric, geriatric, psychiatric, or secondary gain scenarios), self-reporting may be unreliable and objective findings should be weighted more heavily.

nodes.label: No Known Drug Allergies
nodes.description: Assumes no known drug allergies unless documented in medical history
nodes.assumption: The patient has no known drug allergies (NKDA) beyond what is documented in their medical history. If set to false, all medication recommendations should be cross-referenced against known allergies, and alternatives should be suggested proactively.

nodes.label: Urgency Triage
nodes.description: Classify the presentation as Emergency, Urgent, or Routine based on vitals, pain, and red flags
nodes.condition: Triage the patient's presentation based on vital signs, pain severity, symptom onset, and red flag indicators

nodes.label: Red Flag Assessment
nodes.description: Screen for critical warning signs that require immediate intervention
nodes.condition: Evaluate for red flags: sudden worst headache, chest pain with radiation, hemodynamic instability, altered consciousness, acute neuro deficit, signs of sepsis, acute abdomen

nodes.label: Pain & Risk Stratification
nodes.description: Stratify by pain severity and age-related risk factors to determine if pain management is needed before workup
nodes.condition: If pain is ≥7/10 or patient is in a high-risk age group (pediatric <5, geriatric >75), route through pain management first; otherwise proceed to system-specific workup

nodes.label: System Routing
nodes.description: Route to the appropriate diagnostic workup based on the primary symptom category
nodes.condition: Route to the specific body-system diagnostic pathway based on the patient's primary symptom selection

nodes.label: Chronic vs Acute
nodes.description: Determine whether the confirmed diagnosis represents an acute episode or a chronic condition requiring long-term management
nodes.condition: Is this diagnosis an acute episode that will resolve with treatment, or a chronic/recurring condition requiring ongoing management?

nodes.label: Cardiac Workup
nodes.description: Copilot generates a cardiovascular diagnostic plan based on patient presentation

nodes.label: Neurological Assessment
nodes.description: Copilot generates a neurological diagnostic plan

nodes.label: GI Differential Diagnosis
nodes.description: Copilot generates a gastrointestinal diagnostic plan

nodes.label: Respiratory Evaluation
nodes.description: Copilot generates a respiratory diagnostic plan

nodes.label: Musculoskeletal Assessment
nodes.description: Copilot generates a musculoskeletal diagnostic plan

nodes.label: Dermatological Differential
nodes.description: Copilot generates a dermatological diagnostic plan with image analysis consideration

nodes.label: Systemic / Infectious Workup
nodes.description: Copilot generates a systemic and infectious disease diagnostic plan

nodes.label: Emergency Triage Protocol
nodes.description: Copilot generates immediate stabilization and emergency management steps

nodes.label: Pain Management Protocol
nodes.description: Copilot generates a pain management plan prior to full diagnostic workup

nodes.label: Additional Diagnostic Workup
nodes.description: Copilot recommends further testing when initial assessment is inconclusive

nodes.label: Lab Results Interpretation
nodes.description: Copilot interprets laboratory results in the context of the clinical presentation

nodes.label: Imaging Review & Analysis
nodes.description: Copilot interprets imaging findings in clinical context

nodes.label: Treatment Plan Generation
nodes.description: Copilot generates a comprehensive treatment plan based on the confirmed diagnosis

nodes.label: Chronic Disease Management
nodes.description: Copilot generates a long-term management plan for chronic or recurring conditions

nodes.label: Medication Interaction Review
nodes.description: Copilot reviews medications for interactions, contraindications, and optimization
nodes.template:
Patient: {{demographics}}
Medical History & Current Medications: {{medicalHistory}}
Allergies: {{noDrugAllergies}}
Primary Symptom: {{primarySymptom}}
Reported Side Effect or Interaction Concern

Perform a comprehensive medication review:
1. Drug-drug interaction check (major, moderate, minor) with clinical significance
2. Drug-disease contraindication check
3. Drug-allergy cross-reactivity assessment
4. Renal/hepatic dose adjustments needed
5. Therapeutic duplication identification
6. Deprescribing opportunities (PIMs per Beers criteria if elderly)
7. Generic substitution options for cost savings
8. Recommended medication changes with rationale
9. Monitoring plan for any adjusted medications

nodes.label: Drug Allergy Assessment
nodes.description: Copilot evaluates reported drug allergy and recommends safe alternatives

nodes.label: Clinical Reassessment
nodes.description: Copilot performs a clinical reassessment when initial treatment is not effective

nodes.label: Mental Health Screening
nodes.description: Copilot screens for mental health comorbidities that may affect physical symptoms

nodes.label: Preventive Care Guidance
nodes.description: Copilot provides preventive care recommendations based on age, sex, and risk factors

nodes.label: Specialist Referral
nodes.description: Terminal — generates a specialist referral recommendation with handoff summary

nodes.label: ✅ Case Resolved
nodes.description: Terminal — the clinical concern has been addressed and the patient is on a clear management path
nodes.template:
Patient: {{demographics}}
Presenting Symptom: {{primarySymptom}}
Outcome: Clinical concern addressed
Management Plan: In place

Generate a discharge/visit summary:
1. Final diagnosis or working diagnosis
2. Treatment plan summary
3. Medications prescribed (with patient-friendly instructions)
4. Follow-up appointments scheduled
5. Warning signs that should trigger return to care
6. Patient education resources
7. Lifestyle recommendations
8. Expected recovery timeline

nodes.label: 🚨 Emergency Escalation
nodes.description: Terminal — patient requires immediate emergency department transfer or higher level of care

edges.label: ✗ Refractory Pain — Refer to Pain Specialist
